pi_items.source: setup_task.sh writes these exact folder names into /home/ga/Desktop/archival_browser_spec.txt and verifier.py strictly enforces checking these substrings against Chrome's exported Bookmarks JSON.
setup_task.sh injects these clutter domains into the Chrome Bookmarks JSON and lists them in the spec file. verifier.py expects them all to be removed.
setup_task.sh writes these flags to the spec file. verifier.py checks 'Local State' for these exact strings in 'enabled_labs_experiments'.
setup_task.sh instructs the creation of a 'Wayback' search engine with shortcut 'ia'. verifier.py queries the Chrome Web Data sqlite database for this keyword.
setup_task.sh instructs the creation of a search engine with URL 'https://web.archive.org/web/*/%s'. verifier.py queries the Chrome Web Data sqlite database for this pattern.
setup_task.sh writes the download path '/home/ga/Documents/Web_Archives' into the spec file. verifier.py checks Chrome 'Preferences' for 'Web_Archives' in 'default_directory'.

privileged_info_summary: The task operates on synthetically generated Chrome bookmarks seeded by setup_task.sh. It explicitly instructs the creation of 4 folders ('Web Archives', 'Metadata Standards', 'Repositories', 'Crawl Tools') and the deletion of 5 specific non-archival domains (netflix.com, reddit.com, buzzfeed.com, twitter.com, x.com). The required Chrome flags are 'save-page-as-mhtml' and 'enable-reader-mode', and the user must configure a custom search engine with the keyword 'ia' pointing to 'archive.org/web' and a download directory matching 'Web_Archives', all of which perfectly match the metadata and verifier checks.
